title: Do you support the U.S. strikes on Iran? Here’s what 300 readers said.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: A Boston.com survey reveals 54% of 350 readers support U.S. strikes on Iran, 43% oppose, and 3% are unsure.
Supporters believe strikes target Iran's regime, which funds terrorism and harms regional stability; many supporters identified as Iranian.
Opponents fear escalation into broader war and deferred domestic issues; some criticize lack of congressional approval for the strikes.

title: Paul Pierce tells Celtics fans to keep expectations in check over Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Paul Pierce advises Celtics fans to temper expectations on Jayson Tatum's performance post-injury return.
Jayson Tatum may return before the 2025-26 NBA regular season ends, with possible minutes restriction due to Achilles recovery.
Tatum's return expected to enhance Celtics' depth, but Jaylen Brown remains primary scoring threat amidst playoff push.

title: Patriots are reportedly releasing WR Stefon Diggs after 1 season in New England
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots released Stefon Diggs after one season, saving nearly $17 million in cap space.
Diggs amassed 1,013 receiving yards and 4 touchdowns in the 2025 season for New England.
Diggs faces legal charges of assault and strangulation, affecting his standing with the team.

title: High court judge denies DiZoglio’s ask for outside attorney in audit lawsuit
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Judge Dalila Argaez Wendlandt of the SJC denied State Auditor Diana DiZoglio's motion for a special attorney in her lawsuit.
Attorney General Andrea Campbell seeks to strike DiZoglio's lawsuit, opposing her efforts to audit Massachusetts Legislature operations.
DiZoglio plans to oppose Campbell's motion, maintaining her legal stance despite the court setback and conflict with the AG.

title: Former school bus driver indicted in Hyde Park crash that killed 5-year-old boy
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Jean Charles indicted on charges including involuntary manslaughter in the Hyde Park crash that killed 5-year-old Lens Joseph.
Charles allegedly missed the correct bus stop, leading to Lens Joseph's fatal accident as he crossed Washington Street.
Transdev, operating Boston's school buses, faced scrutiny for poor driver training and record-keeping after the incident.

title: Wedding photo company’s sudden bankruptcy leaves more than 100 Mass. couples scrambling
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Yours Truly Media filed for Chapter 7 bankruptcy, affecting over 100 couples in Massachusetts and over 478 couples nationwide.
The company, based in California, owes more than $2 million in liabilities, including unpaid photographers and videographers.
Massachusetts couples are struggling to reclaim deposits and secure new photographers before their wedding dates.

title: Plan a beach getaway around a concert at Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Hampton Beach Casino Ballroom's 2026 lineup includes Melissa Etheridge, Clint Black, Lita Ford, and David Lee Roth.
A $600 million redevelopment of Hampton Beach Casino to start after the 2027 summer season.
The new project will feature a 3,500-seat venue, boutique hotel, luxury condos, and a charitable gaming casino.

title: Bucket truck overturns into oncoming traffic; worker seriously injured
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bucket truck overturned into oncoming traffic on Memorial Drive in Cambridge; worker seriously injured at around 9 a.m.
Massachusetts Department of Conservation and Recreation closed Memorial Drive from North Harvard Street to JFK Street.
State Police and OSHA are investigating the crash; roadway expected to remain closed for several hours.

title: Healey congratulates WHOOP on plans to expand, add 600 jobs
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: WHOOP plans to add 600 new jobs, mainly at its Kenmore Square headquarters in Boston.
Founded in 2012, WHOOP operates in 56 countries and plans global scaling with new roles across various functions.
Gov. Maura Healey praised WHOOP's expansion as a vote of confidence in Massachusetts' economy and talent.

title: Here’s the Eagles’ reported asking price for a Patriots A.J. Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Eagles are reportedly asking for a deal similar to Quinnen Williams for wide receiver A.J. Brown.
Philadelphia seeks a first-round pick and second-round pick for considering a trade involving A.J. Brown.
A.J. Brown recorded 78 catches for 1,003 yards and seven touchdowns in the 2025 season with the Eagles.
